untranslatable_reason: Pattern requires visual identification of double-top formation with two peaks of similar height and U-shaped valley between them. This requires pattern recognition beyond point-in-time indicator values. Specifically: (1) detection of twin peaks with similar heights requires multi-bar comparison and peak detection logic not available in allowed indicators; (2) rounding turn/U-shaped valley detection requires shape analysis; (3) volume comparison between left top vs right top requires historical peak identification. The signal also lacks explicit stop-loss and target rules (marked AUTHOR_SILENT), making it impossible to define complete exit conditions.
translation_notes: Attempted partial translation using pivot point (nose reference) as entry trigger with volume confirmation and breakout below pivot as exit. However, this does not capture the core pattern mechanics. A proper implementation would require: (1) a pattern_detected indicator or multi-bar lookback logic for peak detection; (2) volume_left_top vs volume_right_top comparison; (3) explicit stop and target rules from the author. The 72% success rate in bear markets and 19% average decline should be backtested against complete pattern definition.
